PageContent.title: PageContent
PageContent.description: Represents static or dynamic content for various pages or sections of the website, supporting dual languages (English and Urdu).

id.description: Unique identifier for the PageContent entity.

pageKey.description: A unique key used to identify a specific page or section of content (e.g., 'aboutUs', 'homeHero').

titleEn.description: The English title of the content.

titleUr.description: The Urdu title of the content.

bodyEn.description: The main body text of the content in English.

bodyUr.description: The main body text of the content in Urdu.

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp indicating when the content was last updated.

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Defines the details of a memorial service offered by Legacy Stone, including dual-language descriptions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Service entity.

nameEn.description: The English name of the service.

nameUr.description: The Urdu name of the service.

descriptionEn.description: A detailed description of the service in English.

descriptionUr.description: A detailed description of the service in Urdu.

iconUrl.description: URL to an icon image representing the service.

order.description: The display order of the service on the website.

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp indicating when the service details were last updated.

GalleryImage.title: GalleryImage
GalleryImage.description: Represents an image in the website's gallery, including image URLs and bilingual descriptive text.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GalleryImage entity.

imageUrl.description: URL to the high-resolution image for full-screen viewing.

thumbnailUrl.description: URL to the lazy-loaded thumbnail image.

altTextEn.description: Alternative text for the image in English, used for accessibility and SEO.

altTextUr.description: Alternative text for the image in Urdu, used for accessibility and SEO.

captionEn.description: A short caption or title for the image in English.

captionUr.description: A short caption or title for the image in Urdu.

displayOrder.description: The display order of the image within the gallery.

uploadDate.description: Timestamp indicating when the image was uploaded.

ContactMessage.title: ContactMessage
ContactMessage.description: Stores messages submitted through the website's contact form.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ContactMessage entity.

senderName.description: The full name of the person who sent the message.

senderEmail.description: The email address of the sender.

senderPhone.description: The phone number of the sender (optional).

subject.description: The subject line of the message.

messageBody.description: The main content of the message.

submissionDate.description: Timestamp indicating when the message was submitted.

isRead.description: A flag indicating whether the message has been marked as read.

definition.description: Stores static and dynamic content for website pages. Publicly readable, admin write-access. Documents include standard content fields and a 'lastUpdated' timestamp.

params.name: pageContentId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific page content document.

definition.description: Stores details for memorial services offered. Publicly readable, admin write-access. Includes bilingual descriptions and display order.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific service document.

definition.description: Stores metadata for gallery images, including URLs, bilingual alt text, and captions. Publicly readable, admin write-access.

params.name: galleryImageId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific gallery image document.

definition.description: Stores messages submitted via the contact form. Publicly writable (create), but only readable and updatable by administrators. Includes a 'isRead' status.

params.name: messageId
params.description: Unique identifier for a submitted contact message.

definition.description: A collection where the existence of a document signifies that the user (identified by userId) has administrative privileges. Used for Authorization Independence and DBAC.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who is an administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and easily debuggable by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The primary strategy employed is **Structural Segregation** combined with **Authorization Independence via Denormalization** for roles. Each top-level collection groups documents with a homogeneous security posture, simplifying security rules significantly.

1.  **Public Read, Admin Write Collections (`/page_content`, `/services`, `/gallery_images`):**
    *   **Authorization Independence:** These collections do not rely on hierarchical authorization. All documents within them are publicly readable, which means `get()` calls in security rules are entirely avoided for read operations. Write operations for these collections will be restricted to users who are identified as administrators.
    *   **QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** Since all documents in these collections are intended for public display, a simple `allow read: if true;` rule can be applied. This inherently satisfies the QAP requirement as all documents are accessible to the public for listing and reading, without needing to filter on data content for access control.

2.  **Contact Messages Collection (`/contact_messages`):**
    *   **Authorization Independence:** This collection allows anonymous or authenticated users to create documents (submit messages). Read and update access (e.g., marking `isRead`) is restricted to administrators. Like the public collections, no `get()` calls are needed for authorization. A user's ability to create a message is independent of any other document, and an admin's ability to read/update messages is solely based on their admin role.
    *   **QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** For message creation, `allow create: if true;` (or similar for auth.uid presence if required) directly satisfies QAP. For read/update by admins, a rule like `allow read, update: if exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid));` means that admins can list and read all contact messages, fulfilling the QAP without filtering data for access.

3.  **Admin Roles Collection (`/roles_admin`):**
    *   **Authorization Independence/DBAC:** A dedicated collection `/roles_admin/{userId}` is used to store administrator roles. The mere existence of a document at `/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)` grants administrative privileges. This design completely avoids custom claims and `get()` calls to other user-specific data, making rules extremely robust and easy to debug. Authorization logic simply checks for document existence.

By ensuring that authorization logic for any document depends only on `request.auth.uid` (for roles) or is universally `true` (for public data), we achieve strong Authorization Independence. Structural segregation ensures each collection has a clear and consistent security profile, making security rules simple to write, understand, and verify. Denormalization of the admin role status (effectively, the role *is* the document existence) ensures that rules are not complex or reliant on `get()` operations, thereby supporting atomic operations and debuggability.
